Attention Is All You Need Contents Cheatsheet
The Attention Atlas

Attention Is
All You Need

How eight researchers threw away recurrence, kept only attention, and accidentally drew the blueprint for every large language model you use today.

A guided reading of Vaswani et al. (2017), arXiv:1706.03762 · Narrated by The Crafter, in the spirit of Crafting Interpreters · researched July 2026

Contents

  1. The Sequential Bottleneck why reading one word at a time had to end
  2. Words as Vectors tokens, embeddings, meaning as direction
  3. What Attention Really Is query, key, value — a soft lookup
  4. Scaled Dot-Product Attention the one formula, taken apart piece by piece
  5. Multi-Head Attention a committee of specialists, run in parallel
  6. Giving Words a Place positional encoding without recurrence
  7. Assembling the Machine encoder/decoder stack, residuals, FFN, masks
  8. Why Self-Attention Wins path length, parallelism, interpretability
  9. Training & Results Adam, warmup, label smoothing, the BLEU scores
  10. From Transformer to Modern LLMs how one paper became GPT, BERT, and the rest
  11. The Glossary every acronym and term, spelled out
  12. Cheatsheet the whole Transformer on one printable page
How to Read This Book

We build in layers. Chapters 1–2 are the ground you stand on; 3–6 are the one idea — attention — turned over from four angles; 7–9 assemble and train the real machine; 10 connects it to the models you already use, and 11 is a dictionary for every three-letter word we meet. Use → and ← to turn pages, ↑ to come back here. You know what an LLM does; by the end you'll know how it works.
